title: Value Network — NAICS 221122 Electric Power Distribution

sections.title: Part A — CFJ & Top-of-Tree
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `naics_code` | 221122 |
| `naics_title` | Electric Power Distribution |
| `sector` | Utilities (NAICS 22) — Service/Ecosystem |
| **CFJ (industry-level)** | Deliver bulk electrical energy from the transmission interface to end-use premises at regulated voltage, frequency, reliability, and price |
| `top_level` | L7 |
| `vn_type` | Process VN (no Product BOM — output is a delivered service, not an artifact) |

**Grounding:** DSOs receive HV energy from TSO, step down through MV (1–60 kV) and LV (<1 kV), meter it, bill it, and must concurrently run planning, protection, maintenance, and compliance. Germany's 880+ DSOs operate under EnWG + §13/§14a, VDE-AR-N 4100 series, and increasingly procure flexibility/storage to defer reinforcement. Fluence's 250 MW Kupferzell Grid Booster (TransnetBW, 2024–2026) and Bayernwerk/LEW feed-in-socket pilots (2025) confirm BESS as a procured L5 asset for the distribution/transmission interface.

---

sections.title: Part B — L7 & L6 Hierarchy
sections.content:
### L7

| Level | Name | Description |
|---|---|---|
| L7 | Electric Power Distribution Ecosystem | End-to-end delivery of electricity from transmission interface to premises, under regulated reliability/quality |

### L6 — Core process steps (sequential)

| Level | Name | Job family (verb + object) | Serves |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | HV/MV Interface & Step-Down | Transform HV supply into MV distribution voltage | All deliveries |
| L6b | Primary Distribution (MV Network) | Transport energy across MV feeders to secondary substations | All deliveries |
| L6c | Secondary Distribution (LV Network) | Step down to LV and deliver to premises | Residential, small C&I |
| L6d | Connection, Metering & Billing | Meter energy flow and charge network tariffs | All connected customers |
| L6e | Grid Planning & Connection Mgmt | Plan capacity and admit new generators/loads | New connections, reinforcement |
| L6f | **Flexibility & Congestion Mgmt** | Dispatch flexibility/storage to relieve MV/LV constraints | Congested feeders, redispatch, §14a, curtailment defer — **Mein Solar L5 sits here** |

### L6 — Horizontal (cross-cutting)

| Level | Name (H) | Job family |
|---|---|---|
| L6H-PQ | Power Quality & Protection (H) | Maintain voltage, frequency, fault isolation across all core steps |
| L6H-MAINT | Asset Maintenance & Outage Response (H) | Inspect, maintain, and restore assets 24/7 |
| L6H-OT | OT / SCADA / DMS / ADMS (H) | Monitor and control grid state in real time |
| L6H-HSE | HSE & Regulatory Compliance (H) | Ensure EnWG, VDE-AR-N, arbeitsschutz, environmental compliance |
| L6H-CUST | Customer Operations & Market Access (H) | Customer service, outage comms, flex-market prequal |

---

sections.title: Part C — L5 Equipment per L6 (alternatives, conf, source)
sections.content:
Confidence: HIGH = AHRI/industry reports; MEDIUM = analyst/single source; LOW = inference.

### L6a — HV/MV Interface & Step-Down

| L5 | Alternatives (share %) | Conf | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| HV/MV Power Transformers | Siemens Energy 25%, Hitachi Energy 20%, SGB-SMIT 18%, GE Vernova 12%, Other 25% | MED | Transformer market reports |
| HV Circuit Breakers & Switchgear | Siemens 30%, Hitachi Energy 22%, ABB 18%, GE Vernova 12%, Other 18% | MED | ABB/industry trade press |
| Protection Relays (HV) | Siemens Siprotec 28%, Hitachi MicroSCADA 18%, GE Multilin 15%, SEL 20%, Other 19% | HIGH | IEC 61850 vendor surveys |

### L6b — Primary Distribution (MV)

| L5 | Alternatives | Conf | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| MV Switchgear (GIS/AIS, SF6-free) | Siemens Blue 25%, ABB 22%, Schneider GM6 20%, Hitachi Energy 15%, Eaton 10%, Other 8% | MED | DSO tender awards DE |
| MV Distribution Transformers | SGB-SMIT 25%, Siemens 20%, Schneider 15%, ABB 12%, Ormazabal 10%, Other 18% | MED | VIK/BDEW data |
| MV Overhead Lines & Pylons | Bystronic, Europoles, Krampe, regional EPC — fragmented | LOW | inference |
| MV Underground Cables | Nexans 25%, Prysmian 22%, NKT 18%, LS Cable 8%, Other 27% | HIGH | cable-market reports |
| Ring Main Units (RMU) | Schneider RM6 25%, ABB SafePlus 20%, Siemens 18%, Ormazabal 15%, Eaton 10%, Other 12% | MED | |

### L6c — Secondary Distribution (LV)

| L5 | Alternatives | Conf | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| LV Distribution Boards | Schneider 30%, ABB 20%, Hager 18%, Siemens 15%, Eaton 10%, Other 7% | MED | |
| LV Cables & Connectors | Nexans, Prysmian, NKT, Lapp — commodity | LOW | |
| Pole-Mounted / Pad-Mount Transformers | SGB-SMIT, Siemens, Schneider — regional | LOW | |
| Street-Cabinet Smart Controllers | Siemens LV-Grid 30%, Gridhound 15%, Digi-Sensors 10%, Other 45% | LOW | DE pilot programs |

### L6d — Connection, Metering & Billing

| L5 | Alternatives | Conf | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Smart Meters (iMSys) | EMH 28%, Landis+Gyr 22%, Iskraemeco 15%, Itron 12%, PPC 10%, Other 13% | HIGH | BSI iMSys rollout DE |
| MDM / Billing Systems | SAP IS-U 35%, Kisters 15%, Schleupen 12%, In-house 20%, Other 18% | MED | |
| Smart Meter Gateway (SMGW) | PPC 35%, Theben 25%, Sagemcom 15%, EMH 10%, Other 15% | HIGH | BSI certification |

### L6e — Grid Planning & Connection

| L5 | Alternatives | Conf | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Grid Planning Software (load-flow, hosting-capacity) | Siemens PSS/E 25%, DIgSILENT PowerFactory 25%, Neplan 15%, Other 35% | HIGH | industry tool surveys |
| Hosting-Capacity / Feed-in-Socket Platforms | envelio 30%, gridX 15%, VENIOS 10%, envalue 10%, In-house 35% | MED | Bayernwerk/LEW pilots |
| Connection Application Portals | In-house 60%, Next Level 15%, Schleupen 10%, Other 15% | LOW | |

### L6f — Flexibility & Congestion Management — ← **MEIN SOLAR L5 SEGMENT**

| L5 | Alternatives | Conf | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Grid-Scale Battery Storage (Netzbooster / Feeder-Relief BESS)** | Fluence 30%, Tesla Megapack 15%, Sungrow PowerTitan 12%, Wärtsilä 8%, Nidec ASI 5%, Hitachi Energy 5%, **EPC integrators (incl. Mein Solar) 10%**, Other 15% | MED | Fluence-Kupferzell, ess-news.com, EDF 2025 DSO tender |
| Flexibility Aggregation / VPP Platforms | Next Kraftwerke 20%, Entrix 12%, Enspired 8%, Flexpower 8%, Tiko 6%, In-house 30%, Other 16% | MED | |
| Mechanical FACTS / STATCOM / SVC | Hitachi Energy 30%, Siemens Energy 25%, GE Vernova 18%, Mitsubishi 12%, Other 15% | MED | |
| Dynamic Line Rating (DLR) Sensors | LineVision 25%, Ampacimon 20%, Heimdall 15%, Other 40% | LOW | |
| Redispatch 2.0 Software | Siemens Spectrum 25%, GE PowerOn 18%, In-house 30%, Other 27% | LOW | BNetzA redispatch |

### Horizontal L6H L5 (condensed)

| Horizontal | L5 | Sample alternatives |
|---|---|---|
| L6H-PQ | Digital Protection Relays | Siemens, Hitachi, SEL, GE |
| L6H-PQ | Power-Quality Monitors | Dewetron, Elspec, Janitza |
| L6H-MAINT | Condition-Monitoring Systems | Doble, Camlin, Dynamic Ratings |
| L6H-MAINT | Mobile Substations & Tools | Siemens, ABB, Megger |
| L6H-OT | SCADA/DMS/ADMS | Siemens Spectrum, GE GridOS, Hitachi Lumada, Survalent |
| L6H-OT | IEC 61850 Substation Automation | Siemens, Hitachi, GE, SEL |
| L6H-HSE | Cybersecurity (IEC 62443 / BSI KRITIS) | Rhebo, Claroty, Nozomi, Dragos |
| L6H-CUST | Outage & CRM | SAP, Salesforce, In-house |

---

sections.title: Part D — L4 Subsystems under Mein Solar's L5 (Grid-Scale BESS for DSOs)
sections.content:
| L4 | Description | Typical suppliers |
|---|---|---|
| Battery Module Rack | LFP prismatic racks + BMS/BAMS/BCMS | CATL, EVE, BYD, Sungrow |
| Power Conversion System (PCS) | 4-quadrant bidirectional inverter, 97–98% eff., grid-forming capable | SMA Sunny Central Storage, Sungrow, Power Electronics, Ingeteam |
| MV Step-Up Transformer | 0.4/20 kV, ≥5 MVA, container-adjacent | SGB-SMIT, Siemens, Schneider |
| MV Switchgear & Protection | SF6-free RMU + IEC 61850 protection | Schneider, ABB, Siemens |
| Thermal Management | Liquid-cooling loop + HVAC | integrated in container (Sungrow, CATL EnerC) |
| Fire Suppression | NOVEC 1230 / aerosol + deflagration venting | Siemens, Fike, Siex |
| Energy Management System (EMS) | Dispatch optimizer + SoC mgmt + market/DSO setpoint interface | AutoGrid (Schneider), Fluence Mosaic/Nispera, Entrix, In-house |
| SCADA / DSO Uplink | IEC 61850 / IEC 60870-5-104 to DMS | Siemens, Hitachi, Bachmann |
| Civil Works | Reinforced foundation, fencing, lightning protection | Regional civil EPC |
| Grid-Code Certification Dossier | VDE-AR-N 4110 Type B plant cert, FGW TR3/TR8 | TÜV NORD, TÜV SÜD, FGH |

---

sections.title: Part E — Company Position Marking (required)
sections.content:
```
L7  Electric Power Distribution Ecosystem
  L6a  HV/MV Interface & Step-Down
  L6b  Primary Distribution (MV Network)
  L6c  Secondary Distribution (LV Network)
  L6d  Connection, Metering & Billing
  L6e  Grid Planning & Connection Mgmt
  L6f  Flexibility & Congestion Management
    L5  Grid-Scale Battery Storage (Netzbooster / Feeder-Relief BESS)  ← MEIN SOLAR PRIMARY POSITION
    L5  Flexibility Aggregation / VPP Platforms
    L5  Mechanical FACTS / STATCOM / SVC
    L5  Dynamic Line Rating Sensors
    L5  Redispatch 2.0 Software
  L6H-PQ   Power Quality & Protection (H)
  L6H-MAINT Asset Maintenance & Outage Response (H)
  L6H-OT   OT / SCADA / DMS / ADMS (H)
  L6H-HSE  HSE & Regulatory Compliance (H)
  L6H-CUST Customer Operations & Market Access (H)
```

**Mein Solar secondary positions:** none inside this VN — Mein Solar does not supply transformers, switchgear, SCADA, or aggregation software on its own. Co-located PV+BESS appears in NAICS 221114, not here.

---

sections.title: Part F — Output Type Activation (key rows)
sections.content:
| L6 | MV feeder congestion | HV/MV bottleneck | LV voltage-band violations | New IPP/load connection | Curtailment recovery |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L6a Step-Down | x | x | | x | |
| L6b Primary MV | x | x | | x | x |
| L6c Secondary LV | | | x | x | |
| L6d Metering | x | x | x | x | x |
| L6e Planning | x | x | x | x | x |
| **L6f Flex/Congestion (BESS)** | **x** | **x** | **x** | **x** | **x** |

L6f is activated by **all** DSO problem types → commodity-level cross-cutting process, but the L5 alternatives inside it are NOT commodities (Netzbooster BESS requires deep integration expertise). Domain moat sits at the L5, not at the L6 level.

---

sections.title: Part G — Strategic Adjacency (04e / 04f / 04g, condensed)
sections.content:
### 04e — Direct Competence Transfer Scan (LOW/MEDIUM only, whole VN)

| VN Unit | Level | Competence required | Company has? | Adapt effort | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mechanical FACTS / STATCOM / SVC | L5 | MV coupling, power electronics, civil, cert | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Same MV + grid-code skills; static-VAR power electronics stack is new |
| Dynamic Line Rating sensors | L5 | MV field work, OT integration | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Install skills transfer; sensor vendor partnership needed |
| MV Switchgear (retrofit EPC) | L5 | MV install, protection relay config | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | EPC skills apply; not a product-build capability but install-scope fit |
| Hosting-Capacity / Feed-in-Socket platforms | L5 | Grid-planning software, ML on load-flow | NO | HIGH | excluded |
| Redispatch 2.0 software | L5 | Utility IT, regulated IT procurement | NO | HIGH | excluded |
| Smart Meter Gateway / MDM | L5 | BSI-certified firmware, utility IT | NO | HIGH | excluded |

### 04f — Strategic Adjacency Expansion (within L6f segment only)

Mein Solar's L6f sibling L5 units:

| Adjacent L5 | Current incumbents | Competence gap | Invest to bridge | Fit 1–10 | Rationale (overlap %) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mechanical FACTS / STATCOM / SVC | Hitachi Energy, Siemens Energy, GE, Mitsubishi | Power-electronics product line, reactive-power algorithms | 18–24 mo, €3–5M | 5 | ~40% overlap (MV + civil + cert); product IP is gap — fit as EPC partner, not OEM |
| Flexibility Aggregation / VPP | Next Kraftwerke, Entrix, Enspired, Flexpower, Tiko | Wholesale/BM trading desk, software platform | 24–36 mo, >€5M | 3 | ~15% overlap; pure software+trading play — excluded (below 30% threshold) |
| Dynamic Line Rating sensors | LineVision, Ampacimon, Heimdall | Sensor hardware + pattern analytics | 12–18 mo, €1–2M | 4 | ~35% overlap (MV install, OT interface); product-OEM pivot required |
| Redispatch 2.0 Software | Siemens, GE, in-house utilities | Utility IT, regulated procurement, long sales cycle | >24 mo, >€3M | 2 | <15% overlap — excluded |

**Net:** one credible L6f adjacency (FACTS/STATCOM as EPC partner of power-electronics OEM); remainder is software/trading and is off-mission.

### 04g — Vertical Move-Up (L5 → L6f)

| Current | Target | What must be added | Incumbents at L6f | Feasibility | Economic rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L5 Netzbooster BESS (one of several alternatives) | L6f Flexibility & Congestion Mgmt integrator (owns the full DSO flex stack) | Aggregation/VPP platform, STATCOM line, DLR sensing, redispatch software, utility-grade trading/IT | Siemens Grid Software, Hitachi Energy, Fluence Digital, Next Kraftwerke (aggregation slice) | **LOW** | L6f TAM ~10× L5-BESS TAM in DE, but move-up requires utility-IT + trading-desk + software-OEM org layer Mein Solar does not have. Organic move is blocked; partner-of-record role inside a Siemens/Hitachi-led consortium is the realistic path. |

---

sections.title: Part H — QA Checklist (7 hard/soft checks)
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | CFJ industry-level (no Mein Solar, no BESS ref) | PASS |
| Q2 | No orphan nodes | PASS |
| Q3 | Valid L7→L6→L5→L4 transitions | PASS |
| Q4 | ≥2 L4 children per L5 (spot-check L6f BESS: 10) | PASS |
| Q5 | L7/L6 not solution-specific | PASS |
| Q6 | Three-question naming test (spot 5) | PASS (short, industry-standard) |
| Q7 | Unit count in 15–60 range | PASS (≈38 units incl. L4) |

---

rows.Name: Electric Power Distribution Ecosystem
